task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas, semicolons, and spaces, treats any run of consecutive delimiters, mixed or not, as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: Code meets all requirements with minor room for improvement

trace: To solve this task, I first created a list of indexes where the delimiters appear or there's a transition from non-delimiter to delimiter. After that, I split the string at these indexes and trimmed the pieces to remove any leading/trailing whitespaces. Lastly, I joined the remaining non-empty pieces with a single comma between each. The edge case mentioned is when an empty string is input; in this instance, it returns an empty string instead of a single comma.
